title: calamintha nepeta subsp. spruneri
intro: Calamintha nepeta subsp. spruneri, also known as mountain mint, is a subspecies of Calamintha nepeta, a flowering plant in the mint family. It is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its fragrant leaves and purple flowers. The medicinal properties of mountain mint include its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating digestive issues, headaches, and skin conditions. The health benefits of mountain mint include its ability to aid digestion, reduce stress, and promote respiratory health, and it can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams.
